title: atriplex nuttallii subsp. buxifolia
intro: Atriplex nuttallii subsp. buxifolia, also known as the desert saltbush, is a plant species in the Amaranthaceae family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The desert saltbush has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of the desert saltbush include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The herbal preparation of the desert saltbush typically involves drying the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a salve or tincture.
